sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes
Requested: Treasury withdrawal to fund Blockfrost's 18-month transition and operations as a free, community-governed not-for-profit public API for Cardano.

Deliverables: Legally establish a not-for-profit entity or sign a host-organization agreement under a preliminary board in Q3 2026., Publish a technical transition architecture and migration plan for community review in Q3 2026., Launch a public usage dashboard in Q3 2026., Hold a board election via on-chain voting under publicly published rules in Q4 2026., Have the newly elected board formally assume its mandate from the preliminary board in Q4 2026., Serve all public API traffic for mainnet, preview, and preprod from the new stack in Q1 2027., Legally transfer Blockfrost source code, trademarks, domain names, and associated assets to the governing entity in Q1 2027., Maintain current service performance throughout the cutover., Open a public sustainability consultation and publish a proposal for community feedback in Q2-Q3 2027., Operate the public API at minimum 99% monthly uptime and publish quarterly reports covering technical metrics and budget summary during 2027.
Deadline/expiry: 18 months of operations including transition period; listed milestones run from Q3 2026 through 2027.

Claim (adoption, proposer_asserted, high materiality): Blockfrost is described as a reliable hosted REST interface that lets developers interact with Cardano without running their own infrastructure or node.

Claim (adoption, independently_verifiable, high materiality): The proposal says Blockfrost usage in the Cardano Foundation developer ecosystem survey grew from 39.3% in 2022 to 71.5% in 2025 and provides links for each year.

Claim (adoption, proposer_asserted, high materiality): The proposal states that last month Blockfrost served 781k unique visitors, more than 1.84 billion API requests, almost 700 requests per second, and over 7 TB of API data.
Claim (adoption, proposer_asserted, high materiality): The proposal states that in most Cardano epochs more than 50% of all transactions are submitted through Blockfrost.
Claim (technical, proposer_asserted, medium materiality): The proposal says more than 100 Icebreakers have joined the effort to decentralize Blockfrost operations.

Claim (economic, proposer_asserted, high materiality): The proposal states that 90% of Cardano traffic served through the Blockfrost API comes from the free tier, making the commercial model difficult for financing public-good operations.
missing: Independent evidence for: Blockfrost is described as a reliable hosted REST interface that lets developers interact with Cardano without running their own infrastructure or node.
Independent evidence for: The proposal states that last month Blockfrost served 781k unique visitors, more than 1.84 billion API requests, almost 700 requests per second, and over 7 TB of API data.
Independent evidence for: The proposal states that in most Cardano epochs more than 50% of all transactions are submitted through Blockfrost.
Independent evidence for: The proposal says more than 100 Icebreakers have joined the effort to decentralize Blockfrost operations.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Treasury analysis
findings: Six-month treasury flow regime: unknown (stale snapshot)
sections.conclusion: Treasury votes require a higher bar because they consume shared ADA and create precedent.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "The proposal says Blockfrost usage in the Cardano Foundation developer ecosystem survey grew from 39.3% in 2022 to 71.5% in 2025 and provides links for each year." and clears the evidence gates.

Strongest NO: a material claim is unsupported — "Blockfrost is described as a reliable hosted REST interface that lets developers interact with Cardano without running their own infrastructure or node." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit.
Strongest hold: a treasury action without a complete deep-research dossier cannot be voted directionally without pretending certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.
